evals.prompt: Trigger a build on the main branch for repo-id-abc123 in project TEST_KEY_456. Use the blocks-deployment skill. The API base URL is https://api.blocks.example.com.
evals.expected_output: POST /cloudbuild/v1/Build/run-build called with repoId: 'repo-id-abc123', projectKey: 'TEST_KEY_456'
expectations: projectKey field is present in the body
Auth header is x-blocks-key: projectKey — NOT Authorization: Bearer
hostingProviderId, regionId, machineConfigId are optional fields

evals.prompt: Monitor build status for build-id-xyz789 in project TEST_KEY_456. Poll until it reaches a terminal state.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Build called with buildId and ProjectKey. Polling stops at Succeeded/Failed/Cancelled.
expectations: Query param uses ProjectKey (PascalCase), not projectKey
Polling interval is ~10 seconds while status is Queued or InProgress
Polling stops when status reaches Succeeded, Failed, or Cancelled
Build status values are correct: Queued, InProgress, Succeeded, Failed, Cancelled

evals.prompt: List all repositories configured in CloudBuild for project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Build/repos called with ProjectKey query param.
expectations: Returns list of repos with repoId, repoUrl, customDeploymentDomain

evals.prompt: Get the available hosting providers, regions, and machine configurations for project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/VcsRepository/HostingConfiguration called with x-blocks-key header.
expectations: Returns hostingProviders[] with nested region[] and machineSpecs[]
Each provider has id and name; each region has id and name; each machine has id, ram, cpu, bandwidth

evals.prompt: Search GitHub repositories for 'frontend' in project TEST_KEY_456. Get page 1 with 30 repos per page.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Github/repos called with Search=frontend, PageNumber=1, PageSize=30, ProjectKey=TEST_KEY_456
expectations: Search query param is present for filtering
PageNumber and PageSize are used for pagination
ProjectKey is PascalCase in query params

evals.prompt: Check if branch 'develop' exists in GitHub repo 'myorg/myapp' for project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Github/GithubBranchExists called with repoId and ProjectKey
expectations: RepoId query param is PascalCase (not repoId lowercase)

evals.prompt: Create a GitHub webhook for repo-id-abc123 in project TEST_KEY_456 to enable auto-deploy on push.
evals.expected_output: POST /cloudbuild/v1/Github/webhook called with x-blocks-key as query parameter
expectations: x-blocks-key is passed as a query parameter — NOT as a header
Auth header is NOT used on this endpoint
Returns webhook config with webhookId, url, events, active

evals.prompt: Update the build settings for repo repo-id-abc123 in project TEST_KEY_456. Set hosting provider to 'aws-1', region to 'us-east-1', machine config to 'small', and custom domain to 'app.example.com'.
evals.expected_output: POST /cloudbuild/v1/Build/repo-settings-update called with all settings
expectations: Request body includes projectKey, repoId, hostingProviderId, regionId, machineConfigId, customDomain
Treats errors as a dictionary (not an array)

evals.prompt: Get build reports for build-id-xyz789 in project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Build/reports called with buildId, type, and ProjectKey
expectations: buildId and ProjectKey are query params

evals.prompt: A build was triggered but failed. The error response is { isSuccess: false, errors: { repoId: 'Repository not found' }, statusCode: 400 }. Explain the error and suggest a fix.
evals.expected_output: Error explanation: repoId does not match any connected repository. Fix: use get-repos to find valid repoId values.
expectations: Correctly identifies that errors is a dictionary (key = field name)
Suggests using get-repos to find valid repository IDs
Does NOT treat errors as an array

evals.prompt: Trigger a manual build (no auto-deploy) for repo-id-abc123 in project TEST_KEY_456. Use the blocks-deployment skill.
evals.expected_output: POST /cloudbuild/v1/Build/manual called with repoId and projectKey
expectations: projectKey field is lowercase in body
Does NOT include hosting settings (optional for manual)

evals.prompt: List all branches for GitHub repository 'myorg/frontend-app' in project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Github/branches called with repo and ProjectKey params
expectations: Returns branches array with name, sha, protected fields

evals.prompt: Get the authenticated GitHub user for project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Github/user called with ProjectKey
expectations: ProjectKey is PascalCase in query param

evals.prompt: Get details for repository repo-id-abc123 in project TEST_KEY_456.
evals.expected_output: GET /cloudbuild/v1/Build/repo-details called with RepoId and ProjectKey

evals.prompt: A user reports their build is stuck in 'Queued' status. Explain what could be causing this and how to check.
evals.expected_output: Explanation of potential causes: system queue backlog, repo not connected, or webhook misconfiguration
expectations: Mentions system queue backlog as common cause
Mentions verifying repo is connected via get-repos
Mentions checking get-build-reports for more details
Mentions polling with get-build to monitor status

evals.prompt: A user gets a 403 error when trying to access the CloudBuild API. What could be wrong and how should they fix it?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of 403 causes: missing cloudadmin role or GitHub not connected
expectations: Mentions checking cloudadmin role in Cloud Portal
Mentions verifying GitHub connection for GitHub endpoints
Does NOT suggest using Authorization: Bearer

evals.prompt: Set up auto-deploy for a new Next.js project. The repo is connected but no webhook exists yet. Walk through the steps.
evals.expected_output: Steps: check hosting config, verify branch, create webhook, trigger test build
expectations: Mentions create-github-webhook with x-blocks-key as query param
Mentions verifying webhook URL matches GitHub settings

evals.prompt: A user wants to deploy to Azure instead of AWS. Their current config has hostingProviderId: 'aws-1'. What API calls are needed?
evals.expected_output: get-hosting-config to list available providers, then update-repo-settings with new provider ID
expectations: Mentions get-hosting-config to find Azure provider IDs
Does NOT suggest hardcoding azure-* IDs

evals.prompt: A build triggered via POST /Build/run-build returned buildId at the top level of the response, not nested in data. Explain why and how to access it.
evals.expected_output: Explanation: BuildResponse adds buildId top-level, not in data wrapper
expectations: Explains BuildResponse envelope adds top-level buildId
Mentions this is unique to trigger endpoints

evals.prompt: Create a complete deployment workflow for a React app: connect repo, configure hosting, set up webhook, trigger build, monitor status.
evals.expected_output: Complete flow using setup-repository-flow and deploy-code-flow
expectations: Mentions correct auth with x-blocks-key header
